Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers
Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input
Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Required: Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Reference DOM (Greenhouse progress dots): Greenhouse-inspired multi-section form with progress indicator dots Reference DOM (Role group not fieldset): Grouped radios use div role=group instead of fieldset Reference DOM (Split phone inputs): Country code select and local number in separate inputs Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: pill-yes-no, aria-labelledby-hidden, split-phone, div-role-textbox, iframe-apply. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Greenhouse progress dots; Role group not fieldset; Split phone inputs.
